rounds.prompt: How many times wider is a human eye than the pupil at full dilation?
rounds.fact: A fully dilated pupil is ~8 mm; the full eyeball is ~24 mm across — about 3× wider. The eyeball barely changes size from birth to adulthood.

rounds.prompt: How many times longer is a human nerve cell than a typical human cell?
rounds.fact: A typical human cell is ~20 micrometres; the sciatic nerve cell can be up to 1 metre long — 50,000× longer. A single cell stretching from your spine to your toe.

rounds.prompt: How many times smaller is a water molecule than a grain of table salt?
rounds.fact: A water molecule is ~0.28 nanometres; a grain of salt is ~0.5 mm — about 1.8 million× larger. A grain of salt contains ~10¹⁸ molecules.

rounds.prompt: How many times thicker is a human fingernail than a spider's silk thread?
rounds.fact: Spider silk is ~4 micrometres thick; a fingernail is ~0.5 mm (500 micrometres) — about 125× thicker. Yet silk is stronger than steel by weight.

rounds.prompt: How many times wider is a human hair than a strand of spider silk?
rounds.fact: Spider silk is ~4 µm wide; human hair is ~70 µm — about 17.5× wider. Yet silk is 5× stronger than steel at the same weight.
